property.name: impala_user
property.description: Impala User.

property.name: impala_log_dir
property.description: impala Log dir

property.name: local_library_dir
property.description: User-defined function (UDF) libraries are copied from HDFS into this local directory.

property.name: enable_ranger
property.description: disable or enable ranger

property.name: enable_trusted_subnets
property.description: disable or enable the --trusted_subnets flag

property.name: impala_ranger_app_id
property.description: Ranger Impala application ID, only applicable if enable_ranger=true

property.name: impala_authorization_provider
property.description: Authorization provider, only applicable if enable_ranger=true

property.name: trusted_subnets
property.description: only applicable if enable_trusted_subnets=true

property.name: enable_admission_control
property.description: Enable Admission control to add adding fair_scheduler_allocation_path and llama_site_path configurations

property.name: impala_proxy_user_config
property.description: Authorized Proxy Users, only applicable if enable_ranger=true. Syntax:‑‑authorized_proxy_user_config=authenticated_user1=delegated_user1,delegated_user2,...;authenticated_user2=... , Eg. : "hue=*;impala=*;hive=* "

property.name: impala_ranger_service_type
property.description: Ranger Service Plugin interface for Impala, only applicable if enable_ranger=true

property.name: impala_scratch_dir
property.description: impala scratch dir

property.name: impala_state_store_port
property.description: IMPALA_STATE_STORE_PORT

property.name: impala_backend_port
property.description: IMPALA_BACKEND_PORT

property.name: impala_daemon_webserver_port
property.description: Web UI port for impalad.

property.name: impala_catalog_webserver_port
property.description: Web UI port for catalogd.

property.name: impala_statestore_webserver_port
property.description: Web UI port for statestored.

property.name: kerberos_reinit_interval
property.description: kerberos_reinit_interval

property.name: mem_limit
property.description: memory limit . etc 70%,10mb,10gb,10tb

property.name: impala_user
property.description: User impala catalog/state-store/daemon runs as

property.name: impala_group
property.description: impala group

property.name: impala_run_init_lib
property.description: Run Ambari IMPALA init_lib helper before daemon start to refresh symlinks in /usr/lib/impala/lib.

property.name: enable_ldap_auth
property.description: Enable Ldap Auth

property.name: impala_ldap_uri
property.description: LDAP/LDAPS URL, only applicable if impala_enable_ldap_auth=true

property.name: ldap_allow_anonymous_binds
property.description: Set true to allow LDAP authentication with a blank password.

property.name: ldap_bind_dn
property.description: Distinguished name of the user to bind as when doing user or group searches. To be used when ldap_allow_anonymous_binds is false and when user or group filters are being used

property.name: impala_ldap_passwords_in_clear_ok
property.description: Allow clear text passwords, only applicable if impala_enable_ldap_auth=true

property.name: impala_ldap_bind_password
property.description: LDAP bind password command providing password to use with --ldap_bind_dn, only applicable if impala_enable_ldap_auth=true

property.name: impala_ldap_domain
property.description: LDAP Domain, only applicable if impala_enable_ldap_auth=true

property.name: ldap_baseDN
property.description: LDAP Base DN, only applicable if impala_enable_ldap_auth=true

property.name: ldap_bind_pattern
property.description: LDAP bind pattern, only applicable if impala_enable_ldap_auth=true

property.name: ldap_ca_certificate
property.description: The location on disk of the certificate, in .pem format, used to confirm the authenticity of the LDAP server certificate. This is the Certificate Authority (CA) certificate, and it was used to sign the LDAP server certificate. If not set, all certificates are trusted, which means that an attacker could potentially intercept otherwise encrypted usernames and passwords.

property.name: ldap_tls
property.description: Enable Ldap TLS

property.name: ldap_user_filter
property.description: A comma separated list of usernames. If specified, users must be on this list for authentication to succeed

property.name: ldap_group_filter
property.description: If specified, users must belong to one of these groups for authentication to succeed.

property.name: ldap_group_dn_pattern
property.description: Colon separated list of patterns for the 'distinguished name' use to search for groups in the directory. Each pattern may contain a '%s' which will be substituted with each group name from --ldap_group_filter when doing group searches.

property.name: ldap_group_membership_key
property.description: The LDAP attribute on group entries that indicates its members.

property.name: ldap_search_bind_authentication
property.description: If set to true, LDAP search bind authentication will be used instead of the default simple bind.

property.name: allow_custom_ldap_filters_with_kerberos_auth
property.description: Set to true if custom ldap filters is enabled with Kerberos auth.

property.name: ldap_group_class_key
property.description: The LDAP objectClass each of the groups in --ldap_group_filter implements in LDAP.

property.name: ldap_user_search_basedn
property.description: The 'distinguished name' DN that will be used to search for the authenticating user, this field is required for search bind authentication.

property.name: ldap_group_search_basedn
property.description: The 'distinguished name' DN that will be used to search for the authenticating group. If left empty, group checks will not be performed.

property.name: impala_disable_kudu
property.description: Disable Kudu Integration

property.name: impala_idle_query_timeout
property.description: Idle query timeout in seconds

property.name: impala_idle_session_timeout
property.description: Idle session timeout in seconds

property.name: impala_use_local_tz_for_unix_timestamp_conversions
property.description: Support for local timezone to unix timestamp conversion

property.name: impala_convert_legacy_hive_parquet_utc_timestamps
property.description: Support for legacy hive parquet UTC timestamp

property.name: client_services_ssl_enabled
property.description: Encrypt communication between clients and Impala using Transport Layer Security (TLS) (formerly known as Secure Socket Layer (SSL)).

property.name: ssl_server_certificate
property.description: The path to the TLS/SSL file containing the server certificate key used for TLS/SSL. Used when Impala is acting as a TLS/SSL server. The certificate file must be in PEM format.

property.name: ssl_private_key
property.description: The path to the TLS/SSL file containing the private key used for TLS/SSL. Used when Impala is acting as a TLS/SSL server. The certificate file must be in PEM format.

property.name: ssl_private_key_password_cmd
property.description: The password for the private key in the Impala TLS/SSL Server Certificate and Private Key file. If left blank, the private key is not protected by a password.

property.name: ssl_client_ca_certificate
property.description: The location on disk of the certificate, in PEM format, used to confirm the authenticity of SSL/TLS servers that the Impala daemons might connect to. Because the Impala daemons connect to each other, this should also include the CA certificate used to sign all the SSL/TLS Certificates. Without this parameter, SSL/TLS between Impala daemons will not be enabled.

property.name: webserver_certificate_file
property.description: The path to the TLS/SSL file containing the server certificate key used for TLS/SSL. Used when Catalog Server Webserver is acting as a TLS/SSL server. The certificate file must be in PEM format.

property.name: webserver_private_key_file
property.description: The path to the TLS/SSL file containing the private key used for TLS/SSL. Used when Catalog Server Webserver is acting as a TLS/SSL server. The certificate file must be in PEM format.

property.name: webserver_private_key_password_cmd
property.description: The password for the private key in the Catalog Server Webserver TLS/SSL Server Certificate and Private Key file. If left blank, the private key is not protected by a password.

property.name: enable_load_balancer
property.description: Enable or Disable Impala load balancer

property.name: impala_load_balancer_host
property.description: Hostname of the load balancer used for Impala daemons.

property.name: enable_legacy_avx_support
property.description: Enable or Disable Legacy AVX support

property.name: load_catalog_in_background
property.description: If true, loads catalog metadata in the background. If false, metadata is loaded lazily (on access). Only effective in CDH 5 and Impala 1.2.4 and higher.

property.name: use_local_catalog
property.description: Enable catalog metadata caching locally

property.name: catalog_topic_mode
property.description: Mode to set coordinators to pull metadata as needed from catalogd and cache it locally. In minimal mode, all coordinators use the metadata on-demand. In mixed mode, only some coordinators are enabled to use the metadata on-demand.

property.name: hms_event_polling_interval_s
property.description: Set to a positive integer to enable automatic invalidate/refresh of metadata and set the polling frequency in seconds. Recommended to use with tools like Hive and spark with value less than 5 seconds.

property.name: load_auth_to_local_rules
property.description: If checked and Impala is using Kerberos, Impala will use the hadoop.security.auth_to_local rules from HDFS configuration. The relevant HDFS configuration is derived from Additional Rules to Map Kerberos Principals to Short Names.

property.name: statestore_subscriber_timeout_s
property.description: The amount of time (in seconds) that may elapse before the connection with the statestore is considered lost.

property.name: catalogd_ha_preemption_wait_period_ms
property.description: The amount of time in milliseconds that the statestore waits for the second catalogd before finalizing active catalog designation.

property.name: state_store_ha_port
property.description: RPC port used for statestore HA communication between the active and standby statestore instances.

property.name: statestore_ha_preemption_wait_period_ms
property.description: The amount of time in milliseconds that the standby statestore waits before promoting itself when the active statestore is unavailable.

property.name: num_metadata_loading_threads
property.description: The number of metadata loading threads (degree of parallelism) to use when loading catalog metadata.

property.name: inc_stats_size_limit_bytes
property.description: Maximum size of incremental stats the catalog is allowed to serialize per table. This limit is set as a safety check, to prevent the JVM from hitting a maximum array limit of 1GB (or OOM) while building the thrift objects to send to impalads. By default, it's set to 200MB

property.name: datastream_sender_timeout_ms
property.description: The time, in ms, that can elapse before a plan fragment will time-out trying to send the initial row batch

property.name: rpc_negotiation_thread_count
property.description: Maximum number of threads dedicated to handling RPC connection negotiations.

property.name: max_result_cache_size
property.description: Maximum number of query results a client may request to be cached on a per-query basis to support restarting fetches. This option guards against unreasonably large result caches requested by clients. Requests exceeding this maximum will be rejected

property.name: accepted_cnxn_queue_depth
property.description: The size of the post-accept, pre-setup connection queue in each thrift server set up to service Impala internal and external connections.

property.name: idle_client_poll_period_s
property.description: The time, in s, Impala polls to check if a client connection is idle and closes it if the connection is idle.When set to 0, idle client connections stay open until explicitly closed by the clients

property.name: minidump_path
property.description: Directory to write minidump files to. This can be either an absolute path or a path relative to log_dir. Set to empty to disable writing minidump files.

property.name: statestore_minidump_path
property.description: Directory to write statestored minidump files to.

property.name: impalad_minidump_path
property.description: Directory to write impalad minidump files to.

property.name: max_minidumps
property.description: Maximum minidump files Impala keeps around at any one time. A zero or negative value is interpreted as "unlimited".

property.name: statestore_max_minidumps
property.description: Maximum statestored minidump files kept at any one time. A zero or negative value is interpreted as unlimited.

property.name: impalad_max_minidumps
property.description: Maximum impalad minidump files kept at any one time. A zero or negative value is interpreted as unlimited.

property.name: default_query_options
property.description: key = value pairs of default query options for impalad, separated by comma

property.name: statestore_default_query_options
property.description: Key=value pairs of default query options for statestored, separated by comma.

property.name: impalad_default_query_options
property.description: Key=value pairs of default query options for impalad, separated by comma.

property.name: statestore_update_frequency_ms
property.description: Frequency (in ms) with which the statestore sends topic updates to subscribers.

property.name: statestore_num_update_threads
property.description: Number of threads used to send topic updates in parallel to all registered subscribers.

property.name: fe_service_threads
property.description: Number of threads available to serve client requests.

property.name: max_log_files
property.description: The maximum number of log files to keep at each severity level

property.name: max_statestore_log_files
property.description: The maximum number of statestored log files to keep at each severity level.

property.name: impalad_max_log_files
property.description: The maximum number of impalad log files to keep at each severity level.

property.name: max_profile_log_file_size
property.description: The maximum size (in queries) of the profile log file before a new one is created.

property.name: max_profile_log_files
property.description: Maximum number of profile log files to retain. The most recent log files are retained. If set to 0, all log files are retained.

property.name: impalad_max_profile_log_file_size
property.description: The maximum size (in queries) of an impalad profile log file before a new one is created.

property.name: impalad_max_profile_log_files
property.description: Maximum number of impalad profile log files to retain.

property.name: profile_log_dir
property.description: The directory in which profile log files are written. If blank, defaults to log_file_dir/profiles

property.name: impalad_profile_log_dir
property.description: The directory in which impalad profile log files are written.

property.name: enable_audit_event_log
property.description: Enables audit event generation by Impala daemons. The audit log file will be placed in the directory specified by 'audit_event_log_dir' parameter.

property.name: max_audit_event_log_files
property.description: Maximum queries that will be represented in each audit event log file

property.name: audit_event_log_dir
property.description: The directory in which Impala Daemon audit event log files are written

property.name: max_audit_event_log_file_size
property.description: The maximum size (in queries) of the Impala Daemon audit event log file before a new one is created.

property.name: disable_pool_max_requests
property.description: Disables all per-pool limits on the maximum number of running requests.

property.name: disable_pool_mem_limits
property.description: Disables all per-pool mem limits.

property.name: default_pool_mem_limit
property.description: Maximum amount of memory (across the entire cluster) that all outstanding requests in this pool can use before new requests to this pool are queued. Specified in bytes, megabytes, or gigabytes by a number followed by the suffix b (optional), m, or g, either uppercase or lowercase.

property.name: default_pool_max_queued
property.description: Maximum number of requests allowed to be queued before rejecting requests. A negative value or 0 indicates requests will always be rejected once the maximum number of concurrent requests are executing. Ignored if fair_scheduler_config_path and llama_site_path are set.

property.name: default_pool_max_requests
property.description: Maximum number of concurrent outstanding requests allowed to run before queueing incoming requests. A negative value indicates no limit. 0 indicates no requests will be admitted. Ignored if fair_scheduler_config_path and llama_site_path are set.

property.name: queue_wait_timeout_ms
property.description: Maximum amount of time (in milliseconds) that a request will wait to be admitted before timing out.

property.name: enable_core_dumps
property.description: Enable or Disable Core Dumps

property.name: enable_insert_events
property.description: Enables insert events in the events processor, i.e., refresh the tables or partitions automatically on other Impala clusters when Impala inserts into a table. Event processing must be turned on for this flag to have any effect.

property.name: impala_service_java_options
property.description: JVM arguments to override across all Impala components. Includes Kerberos Subject credential handling required for HMS SASL connectivity.

property.name: redaction_rules_file
property.description: Absolute path to sensitive data redaction rules. The rules will be applied to all log messages and query text shown in the Web UI and audit records. Query results will not be affected. Refer to the documentation for the rule file format.

property.name: disk_spill_encryption
property.description: Encrypt and verify the integrity of all data spilled to disk as part of a query.

property.name: abort_on_failed_audit_event
property.description: Shutdown Impala if there is a problem recording an audit event.

property.name: lineage_event_log_dir
property.description: The directory in which lineage event log files are written. Setting this flag with enable lineage logging.

property.name: max_lineage_log_file_size
property.description: The maximum size (in queries) of the lineage event log file before a new one is created (if lineage logging is enabled)

property.name: statestore_heartbeat_frequency_ms
property.description: (Advanced) Frequency (in ms) with which the statestore sends heartbeat heartbeats to subscribers.

property.name: statestore_heartbeat_tcp_timeout_seconds
property.description: (Advanced) The time after which a heartbeat RPC to a subscriber will timeout. This setting protects against badly hung machines that are not able to respond to the heartbeat RPC in short order

property.name: is_coordinator
property.description: To map coordinator role.

property.name: is_executor
property.description: To map executor role.

property.name: statestore_max_missed_heartbeats
property.description: Maximum number of consecutive heartbeat messages an impalad can miss before being declared failed by the statestore.

property.name: statestore_num_heartbeat_threads
property.description: (Advanced) Number of threads used to send heartbeats in parallel to all registered subscribers.

property.name: max_cached_file_handles
property.description: Maximum number of HDFS file handles that will be cached. Disabled if set to 0.

property.name: unused_file_handle_timeout_sec
property.description: Maximum time, in seconds, that an unused HDFS file handle will remain in the HDFS file handle cache. When the underlying file for a cached file handle is deleted, the disk space may not be freed until the cached file handle is removed from the cache. Specifying this timeout allows the disk space of deleted files to be freed in a predictable period of time. If set to zero, unused cached HDFS file handles do not time out.

property.name: impala_defaults
property.description: This is the jinja template for /etc/default/impala file

property.name: impala_catalog_content
property.description: This is the jinja template for /etc/default/impala file
property.value: IMPALA_CATALOG_ARGS=" \ -log_dir={{impala_log_dir}} \ -hostname={{current_host_name}} \ -max_log_files={{impala_env['max_log_files']}} \ -state_store_host={{impala_state_store_host}} \ -catalog_service_host={{impala_catalog_host}} \ -hms_event_polling_interval_s={{impala_env['hms_event_polling_interval_s']}} \ -num_metadata_loading_threads={{impala_env['num_metadata_loading_threads']}} \ -inc_stats_size_limit_bytes={{impala_env['inc_stats_size_limit_bytes']}} \ -datastream_sender_timeout_ms={{impala_env['datastream_sender_timeout_ms']}} \ -accepted_cnxn_queue_depth={{impala_env['accepted_cnxn_queue_depth']}} \ -minidump_path={{impala_env['minidump_path']}} \ -max_minidumps={{impala_env['max_minidumps']}} \ -default_query_options={{impala_env['default_query_options']}} \ -idle_query_timeout={{impala_env['impala_idle_query_timeout']}} \ -idle_session_timeout={{impala_env['impala_idle_session_timeout']}} \ -idle_client_poll_period_s={{impala_env['idle_client_poll_period_s']}} \ -statestore_subscriber_timeout_seconds={{impala_env['statestore_subscriber_timeout_s']}} \ {% if enable_catalogd_ha %} \ -enable_catalogd_ha=true \ {% endif %} \ {% if enable_statestored_ha %} \ -enable_statestored_ha=true \ -state_store_2_host={{impala_secondary_state_store_host}} \ -state_store_2_port={{impala_env['impala_state_store_port']}} \ {% endif %} \ -load_catalog_in_background={{impala_env['load_catalog_in_background']}} \ -load_auth_to_local_rules={{impala_env['load_auth_to_local_rules']}} \ -enable_legacy_avx_support={{impala_env['enable_legacy_avx_support']}} \ -enable_insert_events={{impala_env['enable_insert_events']}} \ -redaction_rules_file={{impala_env['redaction_rules_file']}} \ {% if enable_ranger %} \ -server_name={{hive_server_host}} \ -ranger_service_type={{impala_env['impala_ranger_service_type']}} \ -ranger_app_id={{impala_env['impala_ranger_app_id']}} \ -authorization_provider={{impala_env['impala_authorization_provider']}} \ -authorized_proxy_user_config={{impala_env['impala_proxy_user_config']}} \ {% endif %} \ -disable_kudu={{impala_disable_kudu}} \ {% if security_enabled %} \ -kerberos_reinit_interval={{impala_env['kerberos_reinit_interval']}} \ -principal=impala/{{current_host_name}}@{{realm_name}} \ -keytab_file=/etc/security/keytabs/impala.service.keytab \ {% endif %} \ {% if client_services_ssl_enabled %} \ -ssl_server_certificate={{impala_env['ssl_server_certificate']}} \ -ssl_private_key={{impala_env['ssl_private_key']}} \ -ssl_private_key_password_cmd={{impala_env['ssl_private_key_password_cmd']}} \ -ssl_client_ca_certificate={{impala_env['ssl_client_ca_certificate']}} \ -webserver_certificate_file={{impala_env['webserver_certificate_file']}} \ -webserver_private_key_file={{impala_env['webserver_private_key_file']}} \ -webserver_private_key_password_cmd={{impala_env['webserver_private_key_password_cmd']}} \ {% endif %} \ {% if enable_ldap_auth %} \ -enable_ldap_auth=true \ -ldap_uri={{impala_env['impala_ldap_uri']}} \ -ldap_passwords_in_clear_ok={{impala_env['impala_ldap_passwords_in_clear_ok']}} \ {% if ldap_allow_anonymous_binds %} \ -ldap_allow_anonymous_binds={{impala_env['ldap_allow_anonymous_binds']}} \ {% else %} \ -ldap_bind_dn={{impala_env['ldap_bind_dn']}} \ -ldap_bind_password_cmd={{ldap_bind_password_cmd}} \ {% endif %} \ {% if ldap_domain %} \ -ldap_domain={{impala_env['impala_ldap_domain']}} \ {% endif %} \ {% if ldap_basedn %} \ -ldap_basedn={{impala_env['ldap_basedn']}} \ {% endif %} \ {% if ldap_bind_pattern %} \ -ldap_bind_pattern={{impala_env['ldap_bind_pattern']}} \ {% endif %} \ {% if ldap_search_bind_authentication %} \ -ldap_search_bind_authentication={{impala_env['ldap_search_bind_authentication']}} \ -allow_custom_ldap_filters_with_kerberos_auth={{impala_env['allow_custom_ldap_filters_with_kerberos_auth']}} \ -ldap_user_search_basedn={{impala_env['ldap_user_search_basedn']}} \ -ldap_group_search_basedn={{impala_env['ldap_group_search_basedn']}} \ -ldap_user_filter={{impala_env['ldap_user_filter']}} \ -ldap_group_filter={{impala_env['ldap_group_filter']}} \ {% else %} \ -ldap_user_filter={{impala_env['ldap_user_filter']}} \ -ldap_group_filter={{impala_env['ldap_group_filter']}} \ -ldap_group_dn_pattern={{impala_env['ldap_group_dn_pattern']}} \ -ldap_group_membership_key={{impala_env['ldap_group_membership_key']}} \ -ldap_group_class_key={{impala_env['ldap_group_class_key']}} \ {% endif %} \ {% endif %} \ {% if enable_ldap_tls %} \ -ldap_tls=true \ -ldap_ca_certificate={{impala_env['ldap_ca_certificate']}} \ {% endif %} \ {% if new_catalog_items.keys() %} \ {% for key, value in new_catalog_items.items() %} \ -{{ key }}={{ value }} \ {% endfor %} \ {% endif %} \ "

property.name: impala_state_store_args
property.description: This is the jinja template for /etc/default/impala file
property.value: IMPALA_STATE_STORE_ARGS=" \ -log_dir={{impala_log_dir}} \ -hostname={{current_host_name}} \ -max_log_files={{impala_env['max_log_files']}} \ -state_store_port={{impala_env['impala_state_store_port']}} \ -statestore_update_frequency_ms={{impala_env['statestore_update_frequency_ms']}} \ -statestore_num_update_threads={{impala_env['statestore_num_update_threads']}} \ -inc_stats_size_limit_bytes={{impala_env['inc_stats_size_limit_bytes']}} \ -minidump_path={{impala_env['minidump_path']}} \ -max_minidumps={{impala_env['max_minidumps']}} \ -default_query_options={{impala_env['default_query_options']}} \ -idle_query_timeout={{impala_env['impala_idle_query_timeout']}} \ -idle_session_timeout={{impala_env['impala_idle_session_timeout']}} \ -enable_legacy_avx_support={{impala_env['enable_legacy_avx_support']}} \ -enable_insert_events={{impala_env['enable_insert_events']}} \ -statestore_heartbeat_frequency_ms={{impala_env['statestore_heartbeat_frequency_ms']}} \ -statestore_heartbeat_tcp_timeout_seconds={{impala_env['statestore_heartbeat_tcp_timeout_seconds']}} \ -statestore_max_missed_heartbeats={{impala_env['statestore_max_missed_heartbeats']}} \ -statestore_num_heartbeat_threads={{impala_env['statestore_num_heartbeat_threads']}} \ {% if enable_catalogd_ha %} \ -enable_catalogd_ha=true \ -catalogd_ha_preemption_wait_period_ms={{impala_env['catalogd_ha_preemption_wait_period_ms']}} \ {% endif %} \ {% if enable_statestored_ha %} \ -enable_statestored_ha=true \ -statestore_ha_preemption_wait_period_ms={{impala_env['statestore_ha_preemption_wait_period_ms']}} \ -state_store_ha_port={{impala_env['state_store_ha_port']}} \ -state_store_peer_host={{impala_state_store_peer_host}} \ -state_store_peer_ha_port={{impala_env['state_store_ha_port']}} \ {% endif %} \ -redaction_rules_file={{impala_env['redaction_rules_file']}} \ {% if enable_ranger %} \ -server_name={{hive_server_host}} \ -ranger_service_type={{impala_env['impala_ranger_service_type']}} \ -ranger_app_id={{impala_env['impala_ranger_app_id']}} \ -authorization_provider={{impala_env['impala_authorization_provider']}} \ -authorized_proxy_user_config={{impala_env['impala_proxy_user_config']}} \ {% endif %} \ -disable_kudu={{impala_disable_kudu}} \ {% if security_enabled %} \ -kerberos_reinit_interval={{impala_env['kerberos_reinit_interval']}} \ -principal=impala/{{current_host_name}}@{{realm_name}} \ -keytab_file=/etc/security/keytabs/impala.service.keytab \ {% endif %} \ {% if client_services_ssl_enabled %} \ -ssl_server_certificate={{impala_env['ssl_server_certificate']}} \ -ssl_private_key={{impala_env['ssl_private_key']}} \ -ssl_private_key_password_cmd={{impala_env['ssl_private_key_password_cmd']}} \ -ssl_client_ca_certificate={{impala_env['ssl_client_ca_certificate']}} \ -webserver_certificate_file={{impala_env['webserver_certificate_file']}} \ -webserver_private_key_file={{impala_env['webserver_private_key_file']}} \ -webserver_private_key_password_cmd={{impala_env['webserver_private_key_password_cmd']}} \ {% endif %} \ {% if enable_ldap_auth %} \ -enable_ldap_auth=true \ -ldap_uri={{impala_env['impala_ldap_uri']}} \ -ldap_passwords_in_clear_ok={{impala_env['impala_ldap_passwords_in_clear_ok']}} \ {% if ldap_allow_anonymous_binds %} \ -ldap_allow_anonymous_binds={{impala_env['ldap_allow_anonymous_binds']}} \ {% else %} \ -ldap_bind_dn={{impala_env['ldap_bind_dn']}} \ -ldap_bind_password_cmd={{ldap_bind_password_cmd}} \ {% endif %} \ {% if ldap_domain %} \ -ldap_domain={{impala_env['impala_ldap_domain']}} \ {% endif %} \ {% if ldap_basedn %} \ -ldap_basedn={{impala_env['ldap_basedn']}} \ {% endif %} \ {% if ldap_bind_pattern %} \ -ldap_bind_pattern={{impala_env['ldap_bind_pattern']}} \ {% endif %} \ {% if ldap_search_bind_authentication %} \ -ldap_search_bind_authentication={{impala_env['ldap_search_bind_authentication']}} \ -allow_custom_ldap_filters_with_kerberos_auth={{impala_env['allow_custom_ldap_filters_with_kerberos_auth']}} \ -ldap_user_search_basedn={{impala_env['ldap_user_search_basedn']}} \ -ldap_group_search_basedn={{impala_env['ldap_group_search_basedn']}} \ -ldap_user_filter={{impala_env['ldap_user_filter']}} \ -ldap_group_filter={{impala_env['ldap_group_filter']}} \ {% else %} \ -ldap_user_filter={{impala_env['ldap_user_filter']}} \ -ldap_group_filter={{impala_env['ldap_group_filter']}} \ -ldap_group_dn_pattern={{impala_env['ldap_group_dn_pattern']}} \ -ldap_group_membership_key={{impala_env['ldap_group_membership_key']}} \ -ldap_group_class_key={{impala_env['ldap_group_class_key']}} \ {% endif %} \ {% endif %} \ {% if enable_ldap_tls %} \ -ldap_tls=true \ -ldap_ca_certificate={{impala_env['ldap_ca_certificate']}} \ {% endif %} \ {% if new_statestore_items.keys() %} \ {% for key, value in new_statestore_items.items() %} \ -{{ key }}={{ value }} \ {% endfor %} \ {% endif %} \ "

property.name: impala_server_args
property.description: This is the jinja template for /etc/default/impala file
property.value:
IMPALA_SERVER_ARGS=" \ -log_dir={{impala_log_dir}} \ -hostname={{current_host_name}} \ --scratch_dirs={{impala_env['impala_scratch_dir']}} \ --state_store_host={{impala_state_store_host}} \ -mem_limit={{impala_env['mem_limit']}} \ -catalog_service_host={{impala_catalog_host}} \ -state_store_port={{impala_env['impala_state_store_port']}} \ {% if enable_catalogd_ha %} \ -enable_catalogd_ha=true \ {% endif %} \ {% if enable_statestored_ha %} \ -enable_statestored_ha=true \ -state_store_2_host={{impala_secondary_state_store_host}} \ -state_store_2_port={{impala_env['impala_state_store_port']}} \ {% endif %} \ -fe_service_threads={{impala_env['fe_service_threads']}} \ -enable_insert_events={{impala_env['enable_insert_events']}} \ -inc_stats_size_limit_bytes={{impala_env['inc_stats_size_limit_bytes']}} \ -max_log_files={{impala_env['max_log_files']}} \ -max_profile_log_file_size={{impala_env['max_profile_log_file_size']}} \ -max_profile_log_files={{impala_env['max_profile_log_files']}} \ -profile_log_dir={{impala_env['profile_log_dir']}} \ -disable_pool_max_requests={{impala_env['disable_pool_max_requests']}} \ -disable_pool_mem_limits={{impala_env['disable_pool_mem_limits']}} \ -default_pool_max_queued={{impala_env['default_pool_max_queued']}} \ -default_pool_max_requests={{impala_env['default_pool_max_requests']}} \ -default_pool_mem_limit={{impala_env['default_pool_mem_limit']}} \ -queue_wait_timeout_ms={{impala_env['queue_wait_timeout_ms']}} \ -minidump_path={{impala_env['minidump_path']}} \ -max_minidumps={{impala_env['max_minidumps']}} \ -default_query_options={{impala_env['default_query_options']}} \ -max_result_cache_size={{impala_env['max_result_cache_size']}} \ -unused_file_handle_timeout_sec={{impala_env['unused_file_handle_timeout_sec']}} \ -max_cached_file_handles={{impala_env['max_cached_file_handles']}} \ -max_audit_event_log_file_size={{impala_env['max_audit_event_log_file_size']}} \ -accepted_cnxn_queue_depth={{impala_env['accepted_cnxn_queue_depth']}} \ -idle_client_poll_period_s={{impala_env['idle_client_poll_period_s']}} \ -idle_query_timeout={{impala_env['impala_idle_query_timeout']}} \ -idle_session_timeout={{impala_env['impala_idle_session_timeout']}} \ -use_local_tz_for_unix_timestamp_conversions={{impala_env['impala_use_local_tz_for_unix_timestamp_conversions']}} \ -convert_legacy_hive_parquet_utc_timestamps={{impala_env['impala_convert_legacy_hive_parquet_utc_timestamps']}} \ -insert_inherit_permissions \ -use_local_catalog={{impala_env['use_local_catalog']}} \ -enable_legacy_avx_support={{impala_env['enable_legacy_avx_support']}} \ -disk_spill_encryption={{impala_env['disk_spill_encryption']}} \ -abort_on_failed_audit_event={{impala_env['abort_on_failed_audit_event']}} \ -lineage_event_log_dir={{impala_env['lineage_event_log_dir']}} \ -max_lineage_log_file_size={{impala_env['max_lineage_log_file_size']}} \ -redaction_rules_file={{impala_env['redaction_rules_file']}} \ -is_coordinator={{impala_env['is_coordinator']}} \ -is_executor={{impala_env['is_executor']}} \ -local_library_dir=local_library_dir \ {% if enable_ranger %} \ -server_name={{hive_server_host}} \ -ranger_service_type={{impala_env['impala_ranger_service_type']}} \ -ranger_app_id={{impala_env['impala_ranger_app_id']}} \ -authorization_provider={{impala_env['impala_authorization_provider']}} \ -authorized_proxy_user_config={{impala_env['impala_proxy_user_config']}} \ {% endif %} \ {% if enable_trusted_subnets %} \ --trusted_subnets={{impala_env['trusted_subnets']}} \ {% endif %} \ -disable_kudu={{impala_disable_kudu}} \ {% if not impala_disable_kudu %} \ -kudu_master_hosts={{kudu_master_hosts}} \ {% endif %} \ -use_statestore \ -state_store_host={{impala_state_store_host}} \ -be_port={{impala_env['impala_backend_port']}} \ {% if security_enabled %} \ -kerberos_reinit_interval={{impala_env['kerberos_reinit_interval']}} \ {% if enable_load_balancer %} \ -principal=impala/{{impala_load_balancer_host}}@{{realm_name}} \ {% else %} \ -principal=impala/{{current_host_name}}@{{realm_name}} \ {% endif %} \ -be_principal=impala/{{current_host_name}}@{{realm_name}} \ -keytab_file=/etc/security/keytabs/impala.service.keytab \ {% endif %} \ {% if client_services_ssl_enabled %} \ -ssl_server_certificate={{impala_env['ssl_server_certificate']}} \ -ssl_private_key={{impala_env['ssl_private_key']}} \ -ssl_private_key_password_cmd={{impala_env['ssl_private_key_password_cmd']}} \ -ssl_client_ca_certificate={{impala_env['ssl_client_ca_certificate']}} \ -webserver_certificate_file={{impala_env['webserver_certificate_file']}} \ -webserver_private_key_file={{impala_env['webserver_private_key_file']}} \ -webserver_private_key_password_cmd={{impala_env['webserver_private_key_password_cmd']}} \ {% endif %} \ {% if enable_audit_event_log %} \ -enable_audit_event_log={{impala_env['enable_audit_event_log']}} \ -max_audit_event_log_file_size={{impala_env['max_audit_event_log_file_size']}} \ -audit_event_log_dir={{impala_env['audit_event_log_dir']}} \ {% endif %} \ {% if enable_ldap_auth %} \ -enable_ldap_auth=true \ -ldap_uri={{impala_env['impala_ldap_uri']}} \ -ldap_passwords_in_clear_ok={{impala_env['impala_ldap_passwords_in_clear_ok']}} \ {% if ldap_allow_anonymous_binds %} \ -ldap_allow_anonymous_binds={{impala_env['ldap_allow_anonymous_binds']}} \ {% else %} \ -ldap_bind_dn={{impala_env['ldap_bind_dn']}} \ -ldap_bind_password_cmd={{ldap_bind_password_cmd}} \ {% endif %} \ {% if ldap_domain %} \ -ldap_domain={{impala_env['impala_ldap_domain']}} \ {% endif %} \ {% if ldap_basedn %} \ -ldap_basedn={{impala_env['ldap_basedn']}} \ {% endif %} \ {% if ldap_bind_pattern %} \ -ldap_bind_pattern={{impala_env['ldap_bind_pattern']}} \ {% endif %} \ {% if ldap_search_bind_authentication %} \ -ldap_search_bind_authentication={{impala_env['ldap_search_bind_authentication']}} \ -allow_custom_ldap_filters_with_kerberos_auth={{impala_env['allow_custom_ldap_filters_with_kerberos_auth']}} \ -ldap_user_search_basedn={{impala_env['ldap_user_search_basedn']}} \ -ldap_group_search_basedn={{impala_env['ldap_group_search_basedn']}} \ -ldap_user_filter={{impala_env['ldap_user_filter']}} \ -ldap_group_filter={{impala_env['ldap_group_filter']}} \ {% else %} \ -ldap_user_filter={{impala_env['ldap_user_filter']}} \ -ldap_group_filter={{impala_env['ldap_group_filter']}} \ -ldap_group_dn_pattern={{impala_env['ldap_group_dn_pattern']}} \ -ldap_group_membership_key={{impala_env['ldap_group_membership_key']}} \ -ldap_group_class_key={{impala_env['ldap_group_class_key']}} \ {% endif %} \ {% endif %} \ {% if enable_ldap_tls %} \ -ldap_tls=true \ -ldap_ca_certificate={{impala_env['ldap_ca_certificate']}} \ {% endif %} \ {% if enable_admission_control %} \ -fair_scheduler_allocation_path=/etc/impala/conf/fair-scheduler.xml \ -llama_site_path=/etc/impala/conf/llama-site.xml \ {% endif %} \ {% if new_server_items.keys() %} \ {% for key, value in new_server_items.items() %} \ -{{ key }}={{ value }} \ {% endfor %} \ {% endif %} \ " IMPALAD_ARGS=" \ -max_audit_event_log_files={{impala_env['max_audit_event_log_files']}} \ {% if enable_trusted_subnets %} \ --trusted_subnets={{impala_env['trusted_subnets']}} \ {% endif %} \ " ENABLE_CORE_DUMPS={{impala_env['enable_core_dumps']}} JAVA_TOOL_OPTIONS={{impala_env['impala_service_java_options']}}
LIBHDFS_OPTS=-Djava.library.path=/usr/odp/current/impala-daemon/lib/native # MYSQL_CONNECTOR_JAR=/usr/share/java/mysql-connector-java.jar # IMPALA_BIN=/usr/odp/current/impala-daemon/sbin # IMPALA_HOME=/usr/odp/current/impala-daemon # HIVE_HOME=/usr/odp/current/hive # HBASE_HOME=/usr/odp/current/hbase # IMPALA_CONF_DIR=/etc/impala/conf # HADOOP_CONF_DIR=/etc/impala/conf # HIVE_CONF_DIR=/etc/impala/conf # HBASE_CONF_DIR=/etc/impala/conf
